crates/brokkr-agent/src/cli/commands.rs.name: start
crates/brokkr-agent/src/cli/commands.rs.doc_comment:
- Contextual information
- JSON output format
- Multiple log levels
Implements structured logging with:

## Logging

- Command line arguments
- Configuration files
- Environment variables
Supports configuration through:

## Configuration

- Performs cleanup operations
- Completes in-progress deployments
- Catches SIGTERM/SIGINT signals
The module implements graceful shutdown handling:

## Signal Handling

```
J --> H
I --> J[Report Status]
H --> I[Apply Objects]
G --> H{Process Deployments}

F --> G[Start Main Loop]
E --> F[Fetch Agent Details]
D --> E[Create HTTP Client]
C --> D[Verify PAK]
B --> C[Wait for Broker]
A[Load Config] --> B[Init Logger]
flowchart TD
```mermaid

## Startup Sequence

4. Starts the main processing loop
3. Verifies broker connectivity
2. Initializes logging
1. Loads configuration
The primary entry point for the agent, which:

```
pub async fn start() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>>
```rust

## Main Command

Implements the command-line interface for the Brokkr agent.

# CLI Commands Module

crates/brokkr-agent/src/k8s/api.rs.name: reconcile_target_state
crates/brokkr-agent/src/k8s/api.rs.signature:
(
    objects: &[DynamicObject],
    client: Client,
    stack_id: &str,
    checksum: &str,
) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>>
crates/brokkr-agent/src/k8s/api.rs.doc_comment:
Reconciles the target state of Kubernetes objects for a stack.

This function:
1. Applies priority resources (Namespaces, CRDs) first to ensure dependencies exist
2. Validates remaining objects against the API server
3. Applies all resources with server-side apply
4. Prunes any objects that are no longer part of the desired state but belong to the same stack
5. Rolls back namespace creation if any part of the reconciliation fails

# Arguments
* `k8s_objects` - List of DynamicObjects representing the desired state
* `k8s_client` - Kubernetes client for API interactions

# Returns
* `Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>>` - Success or error with message

crates/brokkr-agent/src/work_orders/buil.signature:
(
    k8s_client: &K8sClient,
    yaml_content: &str,
    work_order_id: &str,
) -> Result<Option<String>, Box<dyn std::error::Error>>
crates/brokkr-agent/src/work_orders/buil.doc_comment:
Executes a build using Shipwright.

This function:
1. Parses the YAML content to find Build resources
2. Applies Build resources to the cluster
3. Creates a BuildRun
4. Watches the BuildRun until completion
5. Returns the image digest on success or error details on failure

# Arguments
* `k8s_client` - Kubernetes client
* `yaml_content` - Multi-document YAML containing Build and WorkOrder
* `work_order_id` - Work order ID for labeling resources

# Returns
Optional result message (image digest on success)

crates/brokkr-agent/src/work_orders/mod..name: broker
crates/brokkr-agent/src/work_orders/mod..doc_comment:
# Work Orders Module

This module handles the work order lifecycle for the Brokkr agent:
- Fetching pending work orders from the broker
- Claiming work orders for execution
- Executing work based on work type (e.g., builds)
- Reporting completion (success/failure) to the broker

## Work Order Flow

```text
1. Poll broker for pending work orders
2. Claim a work order
3. Apply YAML content (Build + WorkOrder resources)
4. Execute work type handler (e.g., create BuildRun for builds)
5. Watch for completion
6. Report result to broker
```

crates/brokkr-agent/src/work_orders/mod..name: build
crates/brokkr-agent/src/work_orders/mod..doc_comment:
```
6. Report result to broker
5. Watch for completion
4. Execute work type handler (e.g., create BuildRun for builds)
3. Apply YAML content (Build + WorkOrder resources)
2. Claim a work order
1. Poll broker for pending work orders
```text

## Work Order Flow

- Reporting completion (success/failure) to the broker
- Executing work based on work type (e.g., builds)
- Claiming work orders for execution
- Fetching pending work orders from the broker
This module handles the work order lifecycle for the Brokkr agent:

# Work Orders Module

crates/brokkr-agent/src/work_orders/mod..name: is_error_retryable
crates/brokkr-agent/src/work_orders/mod..doc_comment:
Determines if an error is retryable by inspecting the error message.

Non-retryable errors include:
- 404 NotFound (resource doesn't exist)
- 403 Forbidden (permission denied)
- 400 BadRequest (malformed request)
- Validation errors

Retryable errors include:
- 429 TooManyRequests
- 500 InternalServerError
- 503 ServiceUnavailable
- 504 GatewayTimeout
- Network/connectivity errors

crates/brokkr-broker/src/api/mod.rs.name: v1
crates/brokkr-broker/src/api/mod.rs.doc_comment:
# API Module

This module handles the API routes and configurations for the Brokkr Broker.
It includes versioned API endpoints and middleware for authentication and request handling.

## Submodules

- `v1`: Contains the version 1 of the API endpoints.

## Main Functions

- `configure_api_routes`: Sets up the main application router with all API routes.
- `healthz`: Health check endpoint handler.
- `readyz`: Ready check endpoint handler.
- `metrics`: Metrics endpoint handler.

## API Endpoints

### Authentication
- `POST /api/v1/auth/pak`: Verifies a Pre-Authentication Key (PAK).
- Returns: AuthResponse with authentication details.
- Required PAK: Any valid PAK (admin, agent, or generator).

### Agents
- `GET /api/v1/agents`: Lists all agents.
- Returns: Array of Agent objects.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK.
- `POST /api/v1/agents`: Creates a new agent.
- Returns: Created Agent object and initial PAK.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK.
- `GET /api/v1/agents/:id`: Retrieves a specific agent.
- Returns: Agent object.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK or matching Agent PAK.
- `PUT /api/v1/agents/:id`: Updates an existing agent.
- Returns: Updated Agent object.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK or matching Agent PAK.
- `DELETE /api/v1/agents/:id`: Soft deletes an agent.
- Returns: No content on success.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK.
- `GET /api/v1/agents/:id/events`: Lists events for a specific agent.
- Returns: Array of AgentEvent objects.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK or matching Agent PAK.
- `POST /api/v1/agents/:id/events`: Creates a new event for a specific agent.
- Returns: Created AgentEvent object.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK or matching Agent PAK.
- `GET /api/v1/agents/:id/labels`: Lists labels for a specific agent.
- Returns: Array of AgentLabel objects.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK or matching Agent PAK.
- `POST /api/v1/agents/:id/labels`: Adds a new label to a specific agent.
- Returns: Created AgentLabel object.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK only.
- `DELETE /api/v1/agents/:id/labels/:label`: Removes a label from a specific agent.
- Returns: No content on success.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK only.
- `GET /api/v1/agents/:id/annotations`: Lists annotations for a specific agent.
- Returns: Array of AgentAnnotation objects.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK or matching Agent PAK.
- `POST /api/v1/agents/:id/annotations`: Adds a new annotation to a specific agent.
- Returns: Created AgentAnnotation object.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK only.
- `DELETE /api/v1/agents/:id/annotations/:key`: Removes an annotation from a specific agent.
- Returns: No content on success.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK only.
- `GET /api/v1/agents/:id/targets`: Lists targets for a specific agent.
- Returns: Array of AgentTarget objects.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK or matching Agent PAK.
- `POST /api/v1/agents/:id/targets`: Adds a new target to a specific agent.
- Returns: Created AgentTarget object.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK or matching Agent PAK.
- `DELETE /api/v1/agents/:id/targets/:stack_id`: Removes a target from a specific agent.
- Returns: No content on success.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK or matching Agent PAK.
- `POST /api/v1/agents/:id/heartbeat`: Records a heartbeat for a specific agent.
- Returns: No content on success.
- Required PAK: Matching Agent PAK.
- `GET /api/v1/agents/:id/applicable-deployment-objects`: Retrieves applicable deployment objects for a specific agent.
- Returns: Array of DeploymentObject objects.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK or matching Agent PAK.

### Generators
- `GET /api/v1/generators`: Lists all generators.
- Returns: Array of Generator objects.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK.
- `POST /api/v1/generators`: Creates a new generator.
- Returns: Created Generator object and its PAK.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK.
- `GET /api/v1/generators/:id`: Retrieves a specific generator.
- Returns: Generator object.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK or matching Generator PAK.
- `PUT /api/v1/generators/:id`: Updates an existing generator.
- Returns: Updated Generator object.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK or matching Generator PAK.
- `DELETE /api/v1/generators/:id`: Soft deletes a generator.
- Returns: No content on success.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK or matching Generator PAK.

### Stacks
- `GET /api/v1/stacks`: Lists all stacks.
- Returns: Array of Stack objects.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK.
- `POST /api/v1/stacks`: Creates a new stack.
- Returns: Created Stack object.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK or Generator PAK (for self).
- `GET /api/v1/stacks/:id`: Retrieves a specific stack.
- Returns: Stack object.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK or associated Generator PAK.
- `PUT /api/v1/stacks/:id`: Updates an existing stack.
- Returns: Updated Stack object.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK or associated Generator PAK.
- `DELETE /api/v1/stacks/:id`: Soft deletes a stack.
- Returns: No content on success.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK or associated Generator PAK.
- `GET /api/v1/stacks/:id/deployment-objects`: Lists deployment objects for a specific stack.
- Returns: Array of DeploymentObject objects.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK or associated Generator PAK.
- `POST /api/v1/stacks/:id/deployment-objects`: Creates a new deployment object for a specific stack.
- Returns: Created DeploymentObject object.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK or associated Generator PAK.
- `GET /api/v1/stacks/:id/labels`: Lists labels for a specific stack.
- Returns: Array of StackLabel objects.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK, associated Generator PAK, or Agent PAK with target.
- `POST /api/v1/stacks/:id/labels`: Adds a new label to a specific stack.
- Returns: Created StackLabel object.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK or associated Generator PAK.
- `DELETE /api/v1/stacks/:id/labels/:label`: Removes a label from a specific stack.
- Returns: No content on success.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK or associated Generator PAK.
- `GET /api/v1/stacks/:id/annotations`: Lists annotations for a specific stack.
- Returns: Array of StackAnnotation objects.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK, associated Generator PAK, or Agent PAK with target.
- `POST /api/v1/stacks/:id/annotations`: Adds a new annotation to a specific stack.
- Returns: Created StackAnnotation object.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK or associated Generator PAK.
- `DELETE /api/v1/stacks/:id/annotations/:key`: Removes an annotation from a specific stack.
- Returns: No content on success.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK or associated Generator PAK.

### Deployment Objects
- `GET /api/v1/deployment-objects/:id`: Retrieves a specific deployment object.
- Returns: DeploymentObject object.
- Required PAK: Admin PAK, associated Generator PAK, or Agent PAK with target.

### Agent Events
- `GET /api/v1/agent-events`: Lists all agent events.
- Returns: Array of AgentEvent objects.
- Required PAK: Any valid PAK.
- `GET /api/v1/agent-events/:id`: Retrieves a specific agent event.
- Returns: AgentEvent object.
- Required PAK: Any valid PAK.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/api/v1/middlewa.name: auth_middleware
crates/brokkr-broker/src/api/v1/middlewa.signature:
(
    State(dal): State<DAL>,
    mut request: Request<Body>,
    next: Next,
) -> Result<Response, StatusCode>
crates/brokkr-broker/src/api/v1/middlewa.doc_comment:
Middleware function for authenticating requests.

This function extracts the PAK from the Authorization header, verifies it,
and adds the resulting `AuthPayload` to the request's extensions.

# Arguments

* `dal` - The data access layer for database operations.
* `request` - The incoming HTTP request.
* `next` - The next middleware in the chain.

# Returns

A `Result` containing either the response from the next middleware or an error status code.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/api/v1/middlewa.name: verify_pak
crates/brokkr-broker/src/api/v1/middlewa.doc_comment:
Verifies the provided PAK and returns the corresponding `AuthPayload`.

This function checks the PAK against agents, generators, and admin roles
using indexed lookups for O(1) performance instead of O(n) table scans.

# Arguments

* `dal` - The data access layer for database operations.
* `pak` - The Pre-Authentication Key to verify.

# Returns

A `Result` containing either the `AuthPayload` or an error status code.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/api/v1/work_ord.name: default_retryable
crates/brokkr-broker/src/api/v1/work_ord.doc_comment:
- `GET /api/v1/work-order-log/:id` - Get completed work order by ID
- `GET /api/v1/work-order-log` - List completed work orders
### Work Order Log (Read-only history)

- `POST /api/v1/work-orders/:id/complete` - Report work order completion
- `POST /api/v1/work-orders/:id/claim` - Claim a work order
- `GET /api/v1/agents/:id/work-orders/pending` - Get claimable work orders for agent
### Agent Operations

- `DELETE /api/v1/work-orders/:id` - Delete/cancel a work order
- `GET /api/v1/work-orders/:id` - Get work order by ID
- `GET /api/v1/work-orders` - List all work orders
- `POST /api/v1/work-orders` - Create a new work order
### Work Order Management (Admin)

## Endpoints

- Querying work order history from the log
- Completing work orders (success/failure)
- Claiming work orders by agents
- Creating work orders with target agents
This module provides functionality for managing work orders through their lifecycle:

Handles API routes and logic for work orders.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/bin.rs.name: main
crates/brokkr-broker/src/bin.rs.doc_comment:
Main function to run the Brokkr Broker application

This function initializes the application, parses command-line arguments,
and executes the appropriate command based on user input.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/cli/commands.rs.name: serve
crates/brokkr-broker/src/cli/commands.rs.doc_comment:
Function to start the Brokkr Broker server

This function initializes the database, runs migrations, checks for first-time setup,
configures API routes, and starts the server with graceful shutdown support.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/cli/commands.rs.name: rotate_admin
crates/brokkr-broker/src/cli/commands.rs.doc_comment:
Function to rotate the admin key

This function generates a new admin key and updates it in the database.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/cli/mod.rs.name: Cli
crates/brokkr-broker/src/cli/mod.rs.doc_comment:
Brokkr Broker CLI

This CLI provides commands to manage the Brokkr Broker, including serving the broker,
creating agents and generators, and rotating keys.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/agent_annot.name: AgentAnnotationsDAL
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/agent_annot.doc_comment: Handles database operations for Agent Annotations.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/agent_annot.name: create
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/agent_annot.doc_comment:
Creates a new agent annotation in the database.

# Arguments

* `new_annotation` - The new annotation details.

# Returns

The created `AgentAnnotation` or a database error.

# Errors

Returns a `diesel::result::Error` if the database operation fails.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/agent_annot.name: get
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/agent_annot.doc_comment:
Retrieves an agent annotation by its ID.

# Arguments

* `annotation_id` - The UUID of the annotation to retrieve.

# Returns

An `Option<AgentAnnotation>` if found, or `None` if not found.

# Errors

Returns a `diesel::result::Error` if the database operation fails.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/agent_annot.name: list_for_agent
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/agent_annot.doc_comment:

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/agents.rs.name: filter_by_labels
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/agents.rs.signature:
(
        &self,
        labels: Vec<String>,
        filter_type: FilterType,
    ) -> Result<Vec<Agent>, diesel::result::Error>
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/agents.rs.doc_comment:
Filters agents by labels.

# Arguments

* `labels` - A vector of label strings to filter by.
* `filter_type` - Specifies whether to use AND or OR logic for multiple labels.

# Returns

Returns a `Result` containing a `Vec` of matching `Agent`s on success,
or a `diesel::result::Error` on failure.

# SQL Queries

For `FilterType::And`:
```sql
SELECT DISTINCT agents.*
FROM agents
WHERE agents.deleted_at IS NULL
AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM agent_labels WHERE agent_labels.agent_id = agents.id AND agent_labels.label = ?)
AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM agent_labels WHERE agent_labels.agent_id = agents.id AND agent_labels.label = ?)
-- ... (repeated for each label)
```

For `FilterType::Or`:
```sql
SELECT DISTINCT agents.*
FROM agents
INNER JOIN agent_labels ON agents.id = agent_labels.agent_id
WHERE agents.deleted_at IS NULL
AND agent_labels.label IN (?, ?, ...)
```

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/agents.rs.name: filter_by_annotations
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/agents.rs.signature:
(
        &self,
        annotations: Vec<(String, String)>,
        filter_type: FilterType,
    ) -> Result<Vec<Agent>, diesel::result::Error>
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/agents.rs.doc_comment:
Filters agents by annotations.

# Arguments

* `annotations` - A vector of (key, value) pairs to filter by.
* `filter_type` - Specifies whether to use AND or OR logic for multiple annotations.

# Returns

Returns a `Result` containing a `Vec` of matching `Agent`s on success,
or a `diesel::result::Error` on failure.

# SQL Generated (Roughly Equivalent)

For `FilterType::Or`:
```sql
SELECT DISTINCT agents.*
FROM agents
INNER JOIN agent_annotations ON agents.id = agent_annotations.agent_id
WHERE agents.deleted_at IS NULL
AND ((agent_annotations.key = ? AND agent_annotations.value = ?)
OR (agent_annotations.key = ? AND agent_annotations.value = ?)
OR ...)
```

For `FilterType::And`:
```sql
SELECT DISTINCT agents.*
FROM agents
WHERE agents.deleted_at IS NULL
AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM agent_annotations WHERE agent_annotations.agent_id = agents.id AND agent_annotations.key = ? AND agent_annotations.value = ?)
AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM agent_annotations WHERE agent_annotations.agent_id = agents.id AND agent_annotations.key = ? AND agent_annotations.value = ?)
-- ... (repeated for each annotation pair)
```

Note: The actual implementation uses Rust code to perform the filtering,
which may not directly translate to a single SQL query.

Returns a Result containing an Option<DeploymentObject> if found, or a diesel::result::Error on failure.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/deployment_.name: get_target_state_for_agent
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/deployment_.signature:
(
        &self,
        agent_id: Uuid,
        include_deployed: bool,
    ) -> Result<Vec<DeploymentObject>, diesel::result::Error>
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/deployment_.doc_comment:
Retrieves a list of undeployed objects for an agent based on its responsibilities.

This method performs the following steps:
1. Get the list of stacks the agent is responsible for
2. Get all deployment objects for these stacks
3. Filter out objects that have been deployed (have corresponding agent events) if include_deployed is false
4. Sort by sequence_id in descending order

# Arguments

* `agent_id` - The UUID of the agent to get undeployed objects for.
* `include_deployed` - Whether to include objects that have already been deployed.

# Returns

Returns a Result containing a Vec of DeploymentObjects for the agent,
sorted by sequence_id in descending order (most recent first), or a diesel::result::Error on failure.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/deployment_.name: search
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/deployment_.doc_comment:
Searches for deployment objects by checksum.

# Arguments

* `checksum` - The checksum to search for.

# Returns

Returns a Result containing a Vec of DeploymentObjects that match the checksum,
or a diesel::result::Error on failure.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/deployment_.name: get_desired_state_for_agent
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/deployment_.doc_comment:
Retrieves applicable deployment objects for a given agent.

This method fetches deployment objects based on the agent's targets and filters out deleted objects.
The results are sorted by sequence_id in descending order.

# Arguments

* `agent_id` - The UUID of the agent to retrieve applicable deployment objects for.

# Returns

Returns a Result containing a Vec of DeploymentObjects that are applicable for the agent,
sorted by sequence_id in descending order (most recent first), or a diesel::result::Error on failure.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/generators..doc_comment:
Data Access Layer for Generator operations.

This module provides a set of methods to interact with the generators table in the database.
It includes operations for creating, retrieving, updating, and deleting generators,
as well as specialized queries for filtering and updating specific fields.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/generators..doc_comment:
Creates a new generator in the database.

Retrieves a generator by its PAK hash.

This method uses the indexed pak_hash column for O(1) lookups

(

        labels: Vec<String>,
        filter_type: FilterType,
    ) -> Result<Vec<Stack>, diesel::result::Error>
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/stacks.rs.doc_comment:
Filters stacks by labels.

# Arguments

* `labels` - A vector of label strings to filter by.
* `filter_type` - Specifies whether to use AND or OR logic for multiple labels.

# Returns

Returns a Result containing a Vec of matching Stacks on success, or a diesel::result::Error on failure.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/stacks.rs.name: filter_by_annotations
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/stacks.rs.signature:
(
        &self,
        annotations: Vec<(String, String)>,
        filter_type: FilterType,
    ) -> Result<Vec<Stack>, diesel::result::Error>
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/stacks.rs.doc_comment:
Filters stacks by annotations.

# Arguments

* `annotations` - A vector of (key, value) pairs to filter by.
* `filter_type` - Specifies whether to use AND or OR logic for multiple annotations.

# Returns

Returns a Result containing a Vec of matching Stacks on success, or a diesel::result::Error on failure.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/stacks.rs.name: get_associated_stacks
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/stacks.rs.doc_comment:
Retrieves all stacks associated with a specific agent based on its labels, annotations, and targets.

This method uses OR logic when matching labels and annotations, meaning a stack will be included
if it matches any of the agent's labels or annotations.

# Arguments

* `agent_id` - The UUID of the agent.

# Returns

Returns a Result containing a Vec of associated Stacks on success, or a diesel::result::Error on failure.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/templates.r.name: create_new_version
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/templates.r.signature:
(
        &self,
        generator_id: Option<Uuid>,
        name: String,
        description: Option<String>,
        template_content: String,
        parameters_schema: String,
    ) -> Result<StackTemplate, diesel::result::Error>
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/templates.r.doc_comment:
Creates a new version of an existing template.

This method automatically determines the next version number based on
the existing versions for the given generator_id and name combination.

# Arguments

* `generator_id` - Optional generator ID. None for system templates.
* `name` - Name of the template.
* `description` - Optional description.
* `template_content` - The Tera template content.
* `parameters_schema` - JSON Schema for parameter validation.

# Returns

Returns a Result containing the created StackTemplate on success, or a diesel::result::Error on failure.

# Returns

Returns a Result containing a Vec of all StackTemplates on success,
or a diesel::result::Error on failure.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/templates.r.name: list_by_generator
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/templates.r.doc_comment:
Lists all non-deleted stack templates for a specific generator.

# Arguments

* `generator_id` - The UUID of the generator to filter by.

# Returns

Returns a Result containing a Vec of StackTemplates belonging to the generator,
or a diesel::result::Error on failure.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/templates.r.name: get_latest_version
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/templates.r.doc_comment:
Gets the latest version of a template by name and generator_id.

# Arguments

* `generator_id` - Optional generator ID. None for system templates.
* `name` - Name of the template.

# Returns

Returns a Result containing an Option<StackTemplate> with the highest version,
or a diesel::result::Error on failure.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/templates.r.name: list_versions
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/templates.r.doc_comment:
Lists all versions of a template by name and generator_id.

# Arguments

* `generator_id` - Optional generator ID. None for system templates.
* `name` - Name of the template.

# Returns

Returns a Result containing a Vec of all versions of the template,
ordered by version descending.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/templates.r.name: list_for_generator
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/templates.r.doc_comment:
Lists all non-deleted templates for a specific generator.

# Arguments

* `generator_id` - The UUID of the generator.

# Returns

Returns a Result containing a Vec of templates for the generator.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/templates.r.name: list_system_templates
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/templates.r.doc_comment:
Lists all non-deleted system templates (generator_id IS NULL).

# Returns

Returns a Result containing a Vec of system templates.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/templates.r.name: soft_delete
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/templates.r.doc_comment:
Soft deletes a stack template by setting its deleted_at timestamp.

# Arguments

* `template_id` - The UUID of the template to soft delete.

# Returns

Returns a Result containing the number of affected rows (0 or 1).

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/templates.r.name: hard_delete
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/templates.r.doc_comment:
Hard deletes a stack template from the database.

# Arguments

* `template_id` - The UUID of the template to hard delete.

# Returns

Returns a Result containing the number of affected rows (0 or 1).

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/templates.r.name: filter_by_labels
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/templates.r.signature:
(
        &self,
        labels: Vec<String>,
        filter_type: FilterType,
    ) -> Result<Vec<StackTemplate>, diesel::result::Error>
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/templates.r.doc_comment:
Filters templates by labels.

# Arguments

* `labels` - A vector of label strings to filter by.
* `filter_type` - Specifies whether to use AND or OR logic for multiple labels.

# Returns

Returns a Result containing a Vec of matching StackTemplates.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/templates.r.name: filter_by_annotations
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/templates.r.signature:
(
        &self,
        annotations: Vec<(String, String)>,
        filter_type: FilterType,
    ) -> Result<Vec<StackTemplate>, diesel::result::Error>
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/templates.r.doc_comment:
Filters templates by annotations.

# Arguments

* `annotations` - A vector of (key, value) pairs to filter by.
* `filter_type` - Specifies whether to use AND or OR logic for multiple annotations.

# Returns

Returns a Result containing a Vec of matching StackTemplates.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_del.name: DEFAULT_CLAIM_TTL_SECONDS
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_del.doc_comment: Default TTL for acquired deliveries (60 seconds).

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_del.name: WebhookDeliveriesDAL
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_del.doc_comment: Data Access Layer for WebhookDelivery operations.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_del.name: create
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_del.doc_comment:
Creates a new webhook delivery.

# Arguments

* `new_delivery` - The delivery to create.

# Returns

Returns the created WebhookDelivery record.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_del.name: get
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_del.doc_comment:
Gets a webhook delivery by ID.

# Arguments

* `id` - The delivery UUID.

# Returns

Returns the delivery if found.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_del.name: claim_for_broker
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_del.signature:
(
        &self,
        limit: i64,
        ttl_seconds: Option<i64>,
    ) -> Result<Vec<WebhookDelivery>, diesel::result::Error>
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_del.doc_comment:
Claims pending deliveries for broker processing (target_labels is NULL or empty).

This atomically sets acquired_by = NULL (meaning broker) and acquired_until
to current time + TTL.

# Arguments

* `limit` - Maximum number of deliveries to claim.
* `ttl_seconds` - TTL for the claim in seconds.

# Returns

Returns claimed deliveries.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_del.name: claim_for_agent
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_del.signature:
(
        &self,
        agent_id: Uuid,
        agent_labels: &[String],
        limit: i64,
        ttl_seconds: Option<i64>,
    ) -> Result<Vec<WebhookDelivery>, diesel::result::Error>
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_del.doc_comment:
Claims pending deliveries for an agent based on label matching.

The agent can only claim deliveries where it has ALL the required labels.

# Arguments

* `agent_id` - The claiming agent's UUID.
* `agent_labels` - Labels the agent has.
* `limit` - Maximum number of deliveries to claim.
* `ttl_seconds` - TTL for the claim in seconds.

# Returns

Returns claimed deliveries.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_del.name: release_expired
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_del.doc_comment:
Releases expired acquired deliveries back to pending status.

This should be called periodically to recover from crashed workers.

# Returns

Returns the number of released deliveries.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_del.name: process_retries
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_del.doc_comment:
Moves failed deliveries back to pending when retry time is reached.

This should be called periodically to process retries.

# Returns

Returns the number of deliveries moved to pending.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_del.name: mark_success
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_del.doc_comment:
Records a successful delivery.

# Arguments

* `id` - The delivery UUID.

# Returns

Returns the updated delivery.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_del.name: mark_failed
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_del.signature:
(
        &self,
        id: Uuid,
        error: &str,
        max_retries: i32,
    ) -> Result<WebhookDelivery, diesel::result::Error>
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_del.doc_comment:
Records a failed delivery attempt and schedules retry if applicable.

# Arguments

* `id` - The delivery UUID.
* `error` - The error message.
* `max_retries` - Maximum retry attempts allowed.

# Returns

Returns the updated delivery.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_del.name: list_for_subscription
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_del.signature:
(
        &self,
        subscription_id: Uuid,
        status_filter: Option<&str>,
        limit: i64,
        offset: i64,
    ) -> Result<Vec<WebhookDelivery>, diesel::result::Error>
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_del.doc_comment:
Lists deliveries for a subscription with optional filtering.

# Arguments

* `subscription_id` - The subscription UUID.
* `status_filter` - Optional status to filter by.
* `limit` - Maximum number of deliveries to return.
* `offset` - Number of deliveries to skip for pagination.

# Returns

Returns recent deliveries for the subscription.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_del.name: retry
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_del.doc_comment:
Retries a failed or dead delivery.

# Arguments

* `id` - The delivery UUID.

# Returns

Returns the updated delivery, or None if not found or not retriable.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_del.name: cleanup_old
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_del.doc_comment:
Deletes old deliveries based on retention policy.

# Arguments

* `retention_days` - Number of days to retain deliveries.

# Returns

Returns the number of deleted records.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_del.name: get_stats
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_del.doc_comment:
Gets delivery statistics for a subscription.

# Arguments

* `subscription_id` - The subscription UUID.

# Returns

Returns counts of deliveries by status.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_del.name: DeliveryStats
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_del.signature: { pending: i64, acquired: i64, success: i64, failed: i64, dead: i64 }

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_sub.name: WebhookSubscriptionsDAL
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_sub.doc_comment: Data Access Layer for WebhookSubscription operations.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_sub.name: create
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_sub.doc_comment:
Creates a new webhook subscription.

# Arguments

* `new_subscription` - The subscription to create.

# Returns

Returns the created WebhookSubscription record.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_sub.name: get
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_sub.doc_comment:
Gets a webhook subscription by ID.

# Arguments

* `id` - The subscription UUID.

# Returns

Returns the subscription if found.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_sub.name: list
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_sub.doc_comment:
Lists all webhook subscriptions.

# Arguments

* `enabled_only` - If true, only return enabled subscriptions.

# Returns

Returns a list of webhook subscriptions.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_sub.name: get_matching_subscriptions
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_sub.doc_comment:
Gets all enabled subscriptions that match a given event type.

# Arguments

* `event_type` - The event type to match (e.g., "health.degraded").

# Returns

Returns subscriptions that should receive this event type.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_sub.name: update
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_sub.doc_comment:
Updates a webhook subscription.

# Arguments

* `id` - The subscription UUID.
* `update` - The fields to update.

# Returns

Returns the updated subscription.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_sub.name: delete
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_sub.doc_comment:
Deletes a webhook subscription.

# Arguments

* `id` - The subscription UUID.

# Returns

Returns the number of deleted rows.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_sub.name: set_enabled
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_sub.signature:
(
        &self,
        id: Uuid,
        enabled: bool,
    ) -> Result<WebhookSubscription, diesel::result::Error>
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_sub.doc_comment:
Enables or disables a subscription.

# Arguments

* `id` - The subscription UUID.
* `enabled` - Whether to enable or disable.

# Returns

Returns the updated subscription.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_sub.name: matches_event_pattern
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_sub.doc_comment:
Matches an event type against a pattern.

Patterns support:
- Exact match: "health.degraded" matches "health.degraded"
- Wildcard suffix: "health.*" matches "health.degraded", "health.failing", etc.
- Full wildcard: "*" matches everything

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_sub.name: tests
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_sub.doc_comment:
It includes methods for creating, updating, deleting, and querying webhook subscriptions.
This module provides functionality to interact with the webhook_subscriptions table.

Data Access Layer for WebhookSubscription operations.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_sub.name: test_matches_event_pattern_exact
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_sub.doc_comment:
It includes methods for creating, updating, deleting, and querying webhook subscriptions.
This module provides functionality to interact with the webhook_subscriptions table.

Data Access Layer for WebhookSubscription operations.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_sub.name: test_matches_event_pattern_wildcard_suffix
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_sub.doc_comment:
It includes methods for creating, updating, deleting, and querying webhook subscriptions.
This module provides functionality to interact with the webhook_subscriptions table.

Data Access Layer for WebhookSubscription operations.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_sub.name: test_matches_event_pattern_full_wildcard
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/webhook_sub.doc_comment:
It includes methods for creating, updating, deleting, and querying webhook subscriptions.
This module provides functionality to interact with the webhook_subscriptions table.

Data Access Layer for WebhookSubscription operations.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.name: WorkOrdersDAL
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.doc_comment: Data Access Layer for WorkOrder operations.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.name: create
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.doc_comment:
Creates a new work order in the database.

# Arguments

* `new_work_order` - A reference to the NewWorkOrder struct.

# Returns

Returns the created WorkOrder on success, or a diesel::result::Error on failure.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.name: get
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.doc_comment:
Retrieves a work order by its UUID.

# Arguments

* `work_order_id` - The UUID of the work order to retrieve.

# Returns

Returns an Option<WorkOrder> if found, or a diesel::result::Error on failure.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.name: list
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.doc_comment:
Lists all work orders from the database.

# Returns

Returns a Vec of all WorkOrders on success, or a diesel::result::Error on failure.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.name: list_filtered
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.signature:
(
        &self,
        status: Option<&str>,
        work_type: Option<&str>,
    ) -> Result<Vec<WorkOrder>, diesel::result::Error>
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.doc_comment:
Lists work orders filtered by status and/or work type.

# Arguments

* `status` - Optional status filter (PENDING, CLAIMED, RETRY_PENDING).
* `work_type` - Optional work type filter (e.g., "build").

# Returns

Returns a Vec of filtered WorkOrders on success, or a diesel::result::Error on failure.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.name: delete
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.doc_comment:
Deletes a work order by its UUID (hard delete).

Note: This also deletes associated work_order_targets via CASCADE.

# Arguments

* `work_order_id` - The UUID of the work order to delete.

# Returns

Returns the number of affected rows on success, or a diesel::result::Error on failure.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.name: list_pending_for_agent
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.signature:
(
        &self,
        agent_id: Uuid,
        work_type: Option<&str>,
    ) -> Result<Vec<WorkOrder>, diesel::result::Error>
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.doc_comment:
Lists pending work orders that are claimable by a specific agent.

A work order is claimable if:
- Status is PENDING
- At least one of the following conditions is met (OR logic):
- Agent is in the work_order_targets (hard targets)
- Agent has a label matching any of the work order's labels
- Agent has an annotation matching any of the work order's annotations

# Arguments

* `agent_id` - The UUID of the agent requesting claimable work orders.
* `work_type` - Optional work type filter.

# Returns

Returns a Vec of claimable WorkOrders on success, or a diesel::result::Error on failure.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.name: claim
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.signature:
(
        &self,
        work_order_id: Uuid,
        agent_id: Uuid,
    ) -> Result<WorkOrder, diesel::result::Error>
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.doc_comment:
Atomically claims a work order for an agent.

This operation will only succeed if:
- The work order exists
- The work order status is PENDING
- At least one of the following conditions is met (OR logic):
- Agent is in the work_order_targets (hard targets)
- Agent has a label matching any of the work order's labels
- Agent has an annotation matching any of the work order's annotations

# Arguments

* `work_order_id` - The UUID of the work order to claim.
* `agent_id` - The UUID of the agent claiming the work order.

# Returns

Returns the updated WorkOrder on success, or a diesel::result::Error on failure.
Returns NotFound if the work order doesn't exist or can't be claimed.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.name: is_agent_authorized_for_work_order
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.signature:
(
        &self,
        conn: &mut diesel::pg::PgConnection,
        work_order_id: Uuid,
        agent_id: Uuid,
    ) -> Result<bool, diesel::result::Error>
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.doc_comment:
Checks if an agent is authorized to claim a work order using any targeting mechanism.

Returns true if the agent matches via hard targets, labels, or annotations.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.name: release
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.signature:
(
        &self,
        work_order_id: Uuid,
        agent_id: Uuid,
    ) -> Result<WorkOrder, diesel::result::Error>
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.doc_comment:
Releases a claimed work order back to PENDING status.

# Arguments

* `work_order_id` - The UUID of the work order to release.
* `agent_id` - The UUID of the agent releasing the work order (must match claimed_by).

# Returns

Returns the updated WorkOrder on success, or a diesel::result::Error on failure.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.name: complete_success
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.signature:
(
        &self,
        work_order_id: Uuid,
        result_message: Option<String>,
    ) -> Result<WorkOrderLog, diesel::result::Error>
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.doc_comment:
Completes a work order successfully and moves it to the log.

# Arguments

* `work_order_id` - The UUID of the work order to complete.
* `result_message` - Optional result message (e.g., image digest).

# Returns

Returns the created WorkOrderLog entry on success, or a diesel::result::Error on failure.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.name: emit_completion_event
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.doc_comment:
Emits a work order completion event.
Emits `workorder.completed` on success, `workorder.failed` on failure.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.name: complete_failure
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.signature:
(
        &self,
        work_order_id: Uuid,
        error_message: String,
        retryable: bool,
    ) -> Result<Option<WorkOrderLog>, diesel::result::Error>
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.doc_comment:
Completes a work order with failure.

If `retryable` is true and retries remain, the work order is marked as RETRY_PENDING.
If `retryable` is false or max retries exceeded, the work order is moved to the log.

# Arguments

* `work_order_id` - The UUID of the work order that failed.
* `error_message` - The error message describing the failure.
* `retryable` - Whether this failure type can be retried.

# Returns

Returns either:
- `Ok(None)` if the work order was scheduled for retry
- `Ok(Some(WorkOrderLog))` if not retryable or max retries exceeded
- `Err` on database error

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.name: process_retry_pending
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.doc_comment:
Resets RETRY_PENDING work orders to PENDING if their backoff period has elapsed.

This should be called periodically by a background job.

# Returns

Returns the number of work orders reset on success, or a diesel::result::Error on failure.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.name: process_stale_claims
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.doc_comment:
Resets stale claimed work orders to PENDING.

A claim is stale if: claimed_at + claim_timeout_seconds < NOW()

This should be called periodically by a background job.

# Returns

Returns the number of work orders reset on success, or a diesel::result::Error on failure.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.name: add_target
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.doc_comment:
Adds an agent as a target for a work order.

# Arguments

* `new_target` - The new target to add.

# Returns

Returns the created WorkOrderTarget on success, or a diesel::result::Error on failure.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.name: add_targets
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.doc_comment:
Adds multiple agents as targets for a work order.

# Arguments

* `work_order_id` - The UUID of the work order.
* `agent_ids` - A slice of agent UUIDs to add as targets.

# Returns

Returns the number of targets added on success, or a diesel::result::Error on failure.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.name: list_targets
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.doc_comment:
Lists all targets for a work order.

# Arguments

* `work_order_id` - The UUID of the work order.

# Returns

Returns a Vec of WorkOrderTargets on success, or a diesel::result::Error on failure.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.name: remove_target
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.signature:
(
        &self,
        work_order_id: Uuid,
        agent_id: Uuid,
    ) -> Result<usize, diesel::result::Error>
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.doc_comment:
Removes a target from a work order.

# Arguments

* `work_order_id` - The UUID of the work order.
* `agent_id` - The UUID of the agent to remove.

# Returns

Returns the number of targets removed on success, or a diesel::result::Error on failure.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.name: get_log
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.doc_comment:
Retrieves a work order log entry by its UUID.

# Arguments

* `log_id` - The UUID of the log entry to retrieve.

# Returns

Returns an Option<WorkOrderLog> if found, or a diesel::result::Error on failure.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.name: list_log
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.signature:
(
        &self,
        work_type: Option<&str>,
        success: Option<bool>,
        agent_id: Option<Uuid>,
        limit: Option<i64>,
    ) -> Result<Vec<WorkOrderLog>, diesel::result::Error>
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.doc_comment:
Lists work order log entries with optional filtering.

# Arguments

* `work_type` - Optional work type filter.
* `success` - Optional success status filter.
* `agent_id` - Optional agent ID filter.
* `limit` - Optional limit on number of results.

# Returns

Returns a Vec of WorkOrderLog entries on success, or a diesel::result::Error on failure.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.name: add_label
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.doc_comment:
Adds a label to a work order.

# Arguments

* `new_label` - The new label to add.

# Returns

Returns the created WorkOrderLabel on success, or a diesel::result::Error on failure.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.name: add_labels
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.doc_comment:
Adds multiple labels to a work order.

# Arguments

* `work_order_id` - The UUID of the work order.
* `labels` - A slice of label strings to add.

# Returns

Returns the number of labels added on success, or a diesel::result::Error on failure.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.name: list_labels
crates/brokkr-broker/src/dal/work_orders.doc_comment:
Lists all labels for a work order.

# Arguments

* `work_order_id` - The UUID of the work order.

# Returns

Returns a Vec of WorkOrderLabels on success, or a diesel::result::Error on failure.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/db.rs.name: ConnectionPool
crates/brokkr-broker/src/db.rs.doc_comment: Represents a pool of PostgreSQL database connections.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/db.rs.name: create_shared_connection_pool
crates/brokkr-broker/src/db.rs.signature:
(
    base_url: &str,
    database_name: &str,
    max_size: u32,
    schema: Option<&str>,
) -> ConnectionPool
crates/brokkr-broker/src/db.rs.doc_comment:
Creates a shared connection pool for PostgreSQL databases.

# Arguments

* `base_url` - The base URL of the database server (e.g., "postgres://username:password@localhost:5432")
* `database_name` - The name of the database to connect to
* `max_size` - The maximum number of connections the pool should maintain
* `schema` - Optional schema name for multi-tenant isolation

# Returns

Returns a `ConnectionPool` instance containing the created connection pool.

# Panics

This function will panic if:
* The base URL is invalid
* The connection pool creation fails

crates/brokkr-broker/src/db.rs.name: validate_schema_name
crates/brokkr-broker/src/db.rs.doc_comment:
Validates a PostgreSQL schema name to prevent SQL injection.

Schema names must start with a letter and contain only alphanumeric characters and underscores.

# Arguments

* `schema` - The schema name to validate

# Returns

Returns `Ok(())` if valid, or an error message if invalid.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/db.rs.name: ConnectionPool
crates/brokkr-broker/src/db.rs.doc_comment:
For detailed documentation, see the [Brokkr Documentation](https://brokkr.io/explanation/components#database-module).

Database connection pool management using diesel and r2d2.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/db.rs.name: get
crates/brokkr-broker/src/db.rs.doc_comment:
Gets a connection from the pool with automatic schema search_path configuration.

If a schema is configured, this method automatically executes `SET search_path` on the
connection to ensure all queries execute in the correct schema context.

# Returns

Returns a pooled connection ready for use.

# Panics

This method will panic if:
* Unable to get a connection from the pool
* The schema name is invalid
* Failed to set the search path

crates/brokkr-broker/src/db.rs.name: setup_schema
crates/brokkr-broker/src/db.rs.doc_comment:
Sets up a PostgreSQL schema for multi-tenant isolation.

This method creates the schema if it doesn't exist and prepares it for migrations.
It should be called during application startup before running migrations.

# Arguments

* `schema` - The schema name to set up

# Returns

Returns `Ok(())` on success, or an error if schema setup fails.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/utils/backgroun.name: start_webhook_delivery_task
crates/brokkr-broker/src/utils/backgroun.doc_comment:
Starts the webhook delivery worker background task.

This task periodically:
1. Releases expired acquired deliveries back to pending
2. Moves failed deliveries with elapsed backoff back to pending
3. Claims pending deliveries for broker (target_labels is NULL)
4. Attempts to deliver each via HTTP POST
5. Marks deliveries as success or failure (with retry scheduling)

# Arguments
* `dal` - The Data Access Layer instance
* `config` - Configuration for the delivery worker

If not set, returns None (watcher disabled).

crates/brokkr-broker/src/utils/config_wa.name: start_config_watcher
crates/brokkr-broker/src/utils/config_wa.doc_comment:
Starts the configuration file watcher as a background task.

This function spawns a tokio task that watches for changes to the specified
configuration file and triggers configuration reloads with debouncing.

# Arguments

* `config` - The ReloadableConfig instance to reload on changes.
* `watcher_config` - Configuration for the watcher.

# Returns

A handle to the spawned task, or None if the watcher couldn't be started.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/utils/event_bus.name: emit_event
crates/brokkr-broker/src/utils/event_bus.doc_comment:
Emits an event by creating webhook deliveries for all matching subscriptions.

This function:
1. Finds all enabled subscriptions matching the event type
2. Creates a webhook_delivery record for each matching subscription
3. Copies target_labels from subscription to delivery for routing

# Arguments
* `dal` - The Data Access Layer instance.
* `event` - The event to emit.

# Returns
The number of deliveries created.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/utils/matching..name: MatchResult
crates/brokkr-broker/src/utils/matching..signature: { matches: bool, missing_labels: Vec<String>, missing_annotations: Vec<(String, String)> }
crates/brokkr-broker/src/utils/matching..doc_comment: Result of a template-to-stack matching operation.

crates/brokkr-broker/src/utils/matching..name: template_matches_stack
crates/brokkr-broker/src/utils/matching..signature:
(
    template_labels: &[String],
    template_annotations: &[(String, String)],
    stack_labels: &[String],
    stack_annotations: &[(String, String)],
) -> MatchResult
crates/brokkr-broker/src/utils/matching..doc_comment:
Check if a template can be instantiated into a stack.

# Matching Rules

- Template with NO labels/annotations = matches ANY stack (permissive)
- Template WITH labels = ALL labels must exist on stack
- Template WITH annotations = ALL annotations (key-value pairs) must exist on stack

# Arguments

* `template_labels` - Labels attached to the template
* `template_annotations` - Annotations attached to the template (key-value pairs)
* `stack_labels` - Labels attached to the target stack
* `stack_annotations` - Annotations attached to the target stack (key-value pairs)

# Returns

A `MatchResult` indicating whether the template matches and details about any
missing labels or annotations.

and prepares the environment for testing.

* The DATABASE_URL environment variable is not set

crates/brokkr-broker/tests/integration/d.name: test_connection_pool_integration
crates/brokkr-broker/tests/integration/d.doc_comment:
Integration test for the connection pool functionality.

This test performs the following steps:
1. Creates a temporary test database
2. Sets up a connection pool for the test database
3. Runs multiple tests to verify database operations and connection pool behavior
4. Cleans up by dropping the test database

The test covers:
- Creating a table
- Inserting and querying data
- Verifying the maximum number of connections
- Testing connection timeout when exceeding the maximum connections

crates/brokkr-broker/tests/integration/d.name: MIGRATIONS
crates/brokkr-broker/tests/integration/d.doc_comment: Integration tests for multi-tenant schema isolation functionality

crates/brokkr-broker/tests/integration/d.name: test_schema_isolation
crates/brokkr-broker/tests/integration/d.doc_comment:
Test complete data isolation between different schemas

This test verifies that:
- Data created in schema_a is not visible from schema_b
- Each schema maintains its own isolated data
- DAL operations respect schema boundaries

crates/brokkr-broker/tests/integration/d.name: test_schema_auto_provisioning
crates/brokkr-broker/tests/integration/d.doc_comment:
Test automatic schema provisioning on first connection

This test verifies that:
- Schema is created automatically when setup_schema() is called
- Migrations run successfully in the new schema
- The schema is usable for normal operations

crates/brokkr-broker/tests/integration/d.name: test_backward_compatibility_no_schema
crates/brokkr-broker/tests/integration/d.doc_comment:
Test backward compatibility with no schema (public schema)

This test verifies that:
- When no schema is specified (None), operations use the public schema
- Existing functionality continues to work without schema configuration
- This ensures backward compatibility with existing deployments

crates/brokkr-broker/tests/integration/d.name: test_invalid_schema_name
crates/brokkr-broker/tests/integration/d.doc_comment:
Test schema name validation

This test verifies that:
- Invalid schema names are rejected
- Only safe schema names (alphanumeric + underscore, starting with letter) are allowed
- SQL injection attempts via schema names are prevented

crates/brokkr-models/src/lib.rs.name: establish_connection
crates/brokkr-models/src/lib.rs.doc_comment:
Establishes a connection to the PostgreSQL database.

This function exists to manage migrations and perform basic testing in this crate
without a specific Data Access Layer (DAL) in place.

# Arguments

* `database_url` - A string slice that holds the URL of the database to connect to.

# Returns

* `PgConnection` - A connection to the PostgreSQL database.

# Panics

This function will panic if it fails to establish a connection to the database.

or providing additional information about the agent that doesn't fit into the main agent structure.

crates/brokkr-models/src/models/agent_ev.doc_comment:
Creates a new `NewAgentEvent` instance.

# Parameters

* `agent_id`: UUID of the agent associated with this event.
* `deployment_object_id`: UUID of the deployment object associated with this event.
* `event_type`: Type of the event. Must be a non-empty string.
* `status`: Status of the event. Must be one of: "SUCCESS", "FAILURE".
* `message`: Optional message providing additional details about the event.

# Returns

Returns `Ok(NewAgentEvent)` if all parameters are valid, otherwise returns an `Err` with a description of the validation failure.

* `name`: Name of the agent. Must be a non-empty string.
* `cluster_name`: Name of the cluster the agent belongs to. Must be a non-empty string.

Returns `Ok(NewAgent)` if both parameters are valid non-empty strings,
otherwise returns an `Err` with a description of the validation failure.

crates/brokkr-models/src/models/audit_lo.name: new
crates/brokkr-models/src/models/audit_lo.signature:
(
        actor_type: &str,
        actor_id: Option<Uuid>,
        action: &str,
        resource_type: &str,
        resource_id: Option<Uuid>,
    ) -> Result<Self, String>
crates/brokkr-models/src/models/audit_lo.doc_comment:
Creates a new audit log entry.

# Arguments
* `actor_type` - Type of actor (admin, agent, generator, system).
* `actor_id` - ID of the actor (None for system).
* `action` - The action performed.
* `resource_type` - Type of resource affected.
* `resource_id` - ID of the affected resource (None if not applicable).

crates/brokkr-models/src/models/deployme.name: new
crates/brokkr-models/src/models/deployme.signature:
(
        agent_id: Uuid,
        deployment_object_id: Uuid,
        status: String,
        summary: Option<String>,
        checked_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    ) -> Result<Self, String>
crates/brokkr-models/src/models/deployme.doc_comment:
Creates a new `NewDeploymentHealth` instance.

# Parameters

* `agent_id`: UUID of the agent reporting health.
* `deployment_object_id`: UUID of the deployment object.
* `status`: Health status (healthy, degraded, failing, unknown).
* `summary`: Optional JSON-encoded health summary.
* `checked_at`: When the health was checked.

# Returns

Returns `Ok(NewDeploymentHealth)` if all parameters are valid,
otherwise returns an `Err` with a description of the validation failure.

crates/brokkr-models/src/models/deployme.signature: { id: Uuid, created_at: DateTime<Utc>, updated_at: DateTime<Utc>, deleted_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>, sequence_id: i64, s...
crates/brokkr-models/src/models/deployme.doc_comment:
- Deployment objects are designed to be immutable after creation, with exceptions for soft deletion.
- The `yaml_checksum` is automatically generated from the `yaml_content`.
- The `yaml_content` must not be empty.
- The `stack_id` must be a valid, non-nil UUID.

## Constraints

tracking the evolution of a stack's configuration.
They provide a historical record of deployments and can be used for rollbacks, audits, and
Deployment objects are used to track changes and updates to stack configurations over time.

## Usage

- `generator_id`: UUID, ID of the generator associated with this deployment object
- `is_deletion_marker`: BOOLEAN, indicates if this object marks a deletion
- `submitted_at`: TIMESTAMP, when the deployment was submitted
- `yaml_checksum`: VARCHAR(64), SHA-256 checksum of the YAML content
- `yaml_content`: TEXT, the YAML content of the deployment
- `stack_id`: UUID, foreign key referencing the `stacks` table
- `sequence_id`: BIGSERIAL, auto-incrementing sequence number for ordering
- `deleted_at`: TIMESTAMP, for soft deletion support
- `updated_at`: TIMESTAMP, when the deployment object was last updated
- `created_at`: TIMESTAMP, when the deployment object was created
- `id`: UUID, primary key

They are stored in the `deployment_objects` table with the following structure:
Deployment objects represent individual deployments or changes to a stack's configuration.

## Data Model

This module defines structures and methods for managing deployment objects in the system.

# Deployment Object Module

crates/brokkr-models/src/models/deployme.name: new
crates/brokkr-models/src/models/deployme.signature:
(
        stack_id: Uuid,
        yaml_content: String,
        is_deletion_marker: bool,
    ) -> Result<Self, String>
crates/brokkr-models/src/models/deployme.doc_comment:
Creates a new `NewDeploymentObject` instance.

# Parameters

* `stack_id`: UUID of the stack this deployment object belongs to.
* `yaml_content`: YAML content of the deployment. Must be a non-empty string.
* `is_deletion_marker`: Boolean indicating if this object marks a deletion.
* `generator_id`: UUID of the generator associated with this deployment object.

# Returns

Returns `Ok(NewDeploymentObject)` if all parameters are valid,
otherwise returns an `Err` with a description of the validation failure.

- `sequence_id`: BIGSERIAL, auto-incrementing sequence number for ordering
- `deleted_at`: TIMESTAMP, for soft deletion support
- `updated_at`: TIMESTAMP, when the deployment object was last updated
- `created_at`: TIMESTAMP, when the deployment object was created
- `id`: UUID, primary key

They are stored in the `deployment_objects` table with the following structure:
Deployment objects represent individual deployments or changes to a stack's configuration.

## Data Model

This module defines structures and methods for managing deployment objects in the system.

# Deployment Object Module

Returns `Ok(NewStackAnnotation)` if all parameters are valid,
otherwise returns an `Err` with a description of the validation failure.

crates/brokkr-models/src/models/stack_te.signature: { id: Uuid, created_at: DateTime<Utc>, updated_at: DateTime<Utc>, deleted_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>, generator_id: Optio...
crates/brokkr-models/src/models/stack_te.doc_comment:
- Unique constraint on (generator_id, name, version).
- The `parameters_schema` must be valid JSON Schema.
- The `template_content` must be valid Tera syntax.
- The `name` must be a non-empty string.

## Constraints

- `checksum`: VARCHAR(64), SHA-256 checksum of template_content
- `parameters_schema`: TEXT, JSON Schema for parameter validation
- `template_content`: TEXT, Tera template content
- `version`: INTEGER, version number (auto-incremented per name+generator_id)
- `description`: TEXT, optional description
- `name`: VARCHAR(255), name of the template
- `generator_id`: UUID, nullable - NULL means system template (admin-only)
- `deleted_at`: TIMESTAMP, for soft deletion support
- `updated_at`: TIMESTAMP, when the template was last updated
- `created_at`: TIMESTAMP, when the template was created
- `id`: UUID, primary key

They use Tera templating syntax and JSON Schema for parameter validation.
Stack templates are reusable definitions for deployment objects with parameter placeholders.

crates/brokkr-models/src/models/stack_te.name: new
crates/brokkr-models/src/models/stack_te.signature:
(
        generator_id: Option<Uuid>,
        name: String,
        description: Option<String>,
        version: i32,
        template_content: String,
        parameters_schema: String,
    ) -> Result<Self, String>
crates/brokkr-models/src/models/stack_te.doc_comment:
Creates a new `NewStackTemplate` instance.

# Parameters

* `generator_id`: Optional generator ID. NULL means system template.
* `name`: Name of the template. Must be non-empty.
* `description`: Optional description. If provided, must not be empty.
* `version`: Version number for this template.
* `template_content`: Tera template content.
* `parameters_schema`: JSON Schema as a string.

# Returns

Returns `Ok(NewStackTemplate)` if all parameters are valid,
otherwise returns an `Err` with a description of the validation failure.

# Note

This constructor performs basic validation. Tera syntax and JSON Schema
validation should be performed separately before calling this.

(
        name: String,

        filters: Option<WebhookFilters>,

crates/brokkr-models/src/models/webhooks.doc_comment:
Creates a new webhook subscription.

# Arguments
* `name` - Human-readable name for the subscription.
* `url_encrypted` - Pre-encrypted webhook URL.
* `auth_header_encrypted` - Pre-encrypted Authorization header (optional).
* `event_types` - List of event types to subscribe to.
* `filters` - Optional filters as WebhookFilters struct.
* `target_labels` - Optional labels for delivery targeting.
* `created_by` - Who is creating the subscription.

# Returns
A Result containing the new subscription or an error.

- Neither `key` nor `value` can contain whitespace.

key-value pairs.
"tier": "production"}, it will be available to any agent that has at least one of those

- `work_order_id`: UUID, foreign key referencing the `work_orders` table

They are stored in the `work_order_annotations` table with the following structure:
Work order annotations are key-value pairs used to target agents by their annotations.

* `label`: The label text. Must be non-empty, max 64 characters, and contain no whitespace.

# Returns

Returns `Ok(NewWorkOrderLabel)` if all parameters are valid,
otherwise returns an `Err` with a description of the validation failure.

crates/brokkr-models/src/models/work_ord.name: WORK_ORDER_STATUS_CLAIMED
crates/brokkr-models/src/models/work_ord.doc_comment:
On completion (success or max retries exceeded), records move to `work_order_log`.

- `RETRY_PENDING`: Failed but waiting for retry backoff period
- `CLAIMED`: Currently being processed by an agent
- `PENDING`: Ready to be claimed by an agent
Work orders transition through these states:

## Status Flow

- `work_order_log`: Permanent audit trail of completed work orders
- `work_orders`: Active queue for routing and retry management
The work order system uses a two-table design:

crates/brokkr-models/src/models/work_ord.name: new
crates/brokkr-models/src/models/work_ord.signature:
(
        work_type: String,
        yaml_content: String,
        max_retries: Option<i32>,
        backoff_seconds: Option<i32>,
        claim_timeout_seconds: Option<i32>,
    ) -> Result<Self, String>
crates/brokkr-models/src/models/work_ord.doc_comment:
Creates a new `NewWorkOrder` instance with validation.

# Parameters

* `work_type`: Type of work (e.g., "build", "test").
* `yaml_content`: Multi-document YAML content.
* `max_retries`: Maximum retry attempts (optional, defaults to 3).
* `backoff_seconds`: Base backoff for retries (optional, defaults to 60).
* `claim_timeout_seconds`: Claim timeout (optional, defaults to 3600).

# Returns

Returns `Ok(NewWorkOrder)` if valid, otherwise `Err` with validation error.

crates/brokkr-utils/src/config.rs.name: StringOrVec
crates/brokkr-utils/src/config.rs.doc_comment:
Default: 60 (set to 0 to disable caching)
- `BROKKR__BROKER__AUTH_CACHE_TTL_SECONDS`: TTL for cached PAK auth results in seconds

Default: "BR"
- `BROKKR__PAK__SHORT_TOKEN_PREFIX`: Sets the prefix for short PAK tokens

Default: 24
- `BROKKR__PAK__LONG_TOKEN_LENGTH`: Sets the length of long PAK tokens

Default: 8
- `BROKKR__PAK__SHORT_TOKEN_LENGTH`: Sets the length of short PAK tokens

Default: 8
- `BROKKR__PAK__DIGEST`: Sets the digest algorithm for PAK generation

Default: "osrng"
- `BROKKR__PAK__RNG`: Sets the random number generator type for PAK generation

Default: "brokkr"
- `BROKKR__PAK__PREFIX`: Sets the prefix for PAKs (Pre-Authentication Keys)

Possible values: "trace", "debug", "info", "warn", "error"
Default: "debug"
- `BROKKR__LOG__LEVEL`: Sets the log level for the application

Example: "tenant_acme"
Default: None (uses public schema)
- `BROKKR__DATABASE__SCHEMA`: Sets the PostgreSQL schema for multi-tenant isolation

Default: "postgres://brokkr:brokkr@localhost:5432/brokkr"
- `BROKKR__DATABASE__URL`: Sets the database connection URL

The following environment variables can be used to configure Brokkr:

# Available Environment Variables

(e.g., `BROKKR__DATABASE__URL=new_value`)
- Using environment variables: Set the variable with the "BROKKR__" prefix and "__" as separators
- In a configuration file: Use the appropriate key (e.g., `database.url = "new_value"`)
To override a configuration value:

3. Environment variables
2. Values from an optional external configuration file (if provided)
1. Default values from the embedded `default.toml` file

The configuration values are loaded and overridden in the following order (later sources take precedence):

# Configuration Overriding

- Configuration file keys use snake_case (e.g., `database.url`, `log.level`)
- Environment variables use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE and are prefixed with "BROKKR__" (e.g., `BROKKR__DATABASE__URL`)
- Struct fields use snake_case (e.g., `database`, `log_level`)
Variables in this configuration framework follow these naming conventions:

# Variable Naming Convention

configuration file format, and best practices, see the [Configuration Guide](https://brokkr.io/getting-started/configuration).

crates/brokkr-utils/src/config.rs.name: new
crates/brokkr-utils/src/config.rs.doc_comment:
Creates a new ReloadableConfig instance

# Arguments

* `file` - An optional path to a configuration file

# Returns

Returns a `Result` containing the `ReloadableConfig` instance or a `ConfigError`

crates/brokkr-utils/src/config.rs.name: from_settings
crates/brokkr-utils/src/config.rs.doc_comment:
Creates a ReloadableConfig from an existing Settings instance

# Arguments

* `settings` - The Settings instance to wrap
* `config_file` - An optional path to the config file for future reloads

# Returns

Returns a `ReloadableConfig` instance

crates/brokkr-utils/src/config.rs.name: static_config

These settings require an application restart to change.

crates/brokkr-utils/src/config.rs.name: reload
crates/brokkr-utils/src/config.rs.doc_comment:

Returns a list of configuration changes that were applied.
Thread-safe: blocks writers during reload.

crates/brokkr-utils/src/logging.rs.name: LOGGER
crates/brokkr-utils/src/logging.rs.doc_comment:
operations and log level changes from multiple threads.
This logger is designed to be thread-safe and can handle concurrent logging

## Thread Safety

- "trace": Log trace messages and all above
- "debug": Log debug messages and all above
- "info": Log info, warnings, and errors (default)
- "warn": Log warnings and errors
- "error": Log only errors
- "off": Turn off all logging
The available log levels are:

## Log Levels

```
brokkr_logger::update_log_level("debug").expect("Failed to update log level");
```
3. Update log level at runtime if needed:

```
error!("This is an error message");
warn!("This is a warning message");
info!("This is an info message");
debug!("This is a debug message");
```
2. Use the log macros throughout your code:

```
brokkr_logger::init("info").expect("Failed to initialize logger");
```
1. Initialize the logger:

## Usage

- Custom log macros for easy use
- Dynamic log level adjustment
- Thread-safe logging
## Features

This module provides a custom logging framework for the Brokkr application.

# Brokkr Logging Module

crates/brokkr-utils/src/logging.rs.name: init
crates/brokkr-utils/src/logging.rs.doc_comment:
Initializes the Brokkr logging system with the specified log level.

Sets up a custom logger that handles structured logging with timestamps,
log levels, and module paths. Supports multiple output formats and
concurrent logging from multiple threads.

# Arguments
* `level` - String representation of the log level ("debug", "info", "warn", "error")

# Returns
* `Result<(), Box<dyn Error>>` - Success/failure of logger initialization

# Example
```
use brokkr_utils::logging;

logging::init("debug")?;
log::info!("Logger initialized successfully");
```

# Features
- Thread-safe logging
- Configurable log levels
- Module path tracking
- Timestamp inclusion
- Error context preservation

# Error Cases
- Invalid log level string
- Logger already initialized
- System permission issues

crates/brokkr-utils/src/logging.rs.name: init_with_format
crates/brokkr-utils/src/logging.rs.doc_comment:
Initializes the Brokkr logging system with the specified log level and format.

# Arguments
* `level` - String representation of the log level ("debug", "info", "warn", "error")
* `format` - Log output format ("text" for human-readable, "json" for structured JSON)

# Returns
* `Result<(), SetLoggerError>` - Success/failure of logger initialization

crates/brokkr-utils/src/logging.rs.name: test_init
crates/brokkr-utils/src/logging.rs.doc_comment:
Verifies that the logger initializes correctly with the specified log level.

This test:
1. Initializes the logger with the "info" level
2. Checks that initialization is successful
3. Verifies that the current log level is set to Info

crates/brokkr-utils/src/logging.rs.name: test_update_log_level
crates/brokkr-utils/src/logging.rs.doc_comment:
Tests the ability to update the log level after initialization.

This test:
1. Initializes the logger with "info" level
2. Updates the log level to "debug" and verifies the change
3. Updates the log level to "warn" and verifies the change

crates/brokkr-utils/src/logging.rs.name: test_invalid_log_level
crates/brokkr-utils/src/logging.rs.doc_comment:
Checks the logger's behavior when given invalid log levels.

This test:
1. Attempts to initialize with an invalid level, expecting it to default to Info
2. Tries to update to another invalid level, expecting no change

crates/brokkr-utils/src/logging.rs.name: test_log_macros
crates/brokkr-utils/src/logging.rs.doc_comment:
Ensures that all log macros can be called without errors.

This test initializes the logger at the debug level and calls each log macro.
It doesn't verify the output, only that the calls don't cause errors.

crates/brokkr-utils/src/logging.rs.name: test_thread_safety_and_performance
crates/brokkr-utils/src/logging.rs.doc_comment:
Tests thread safety and performance of the logger under concurrent usage.

This test:
1. Spawns multiple threads that perform logging operations and level changes
2. Measures the time taken for operations and the maximum time for a single log
3. Verifies that all operations complete without errors or deadlocks
4. Checks that no single log operation takes an unusually long time

crates/brokkr-utils/src/telemetry.rs.name: TelemetryError
fmt
TelemetryError
init
crates/brokkr-utils/src/telemetry.rs.doc_comment:
Initialize OpenTelemetry tracing with the given configuration.

If telemetry is disabled in the config, this function sets up a basic
tracing subscriber without OpenTelemetry export.

# Arguments
* `config` - Resolved telemetry configuration (from `Telemetry::for_broker()` or `for_agent()`)
* `log_level` - Log level filter string (e.g., "info", "debug")
* `log_format` - Log format ("text" or "json")

# Returns
* `Ok(())` on success
* `Err(TelemetryError)` if initialization fails

crates/brokkr-utils/src/telemetry.rs.name: shutdown
crates/brokkr-utils/src/telemetry.rs.doc_comment:
Shutdown OpenTelemetry, flushing any pending traces.

Should be called during graceful shutdown to ensure all traces are exported.

crates/brokkr-utils/src/telemetry.rs.name: tests
test_disabled_telemetry_config
test_sampling_rate_bounds

crates/brokkr-utils/tests/integration.rs.name: test_settings_from_file_and_env
crates/brokkr-utils/tests/integration.rs.doc_comment:
Tests the loading of settings from both a file and environment variables.

This test:
1. Creates a temporary TOML configuration file with specific settings.
2. Sets an environment variable to override one of the settings.
3. Loads the settings using the Settings::new() method.
4. Verifies that settings are correctly loaded from the file.
5. Checks that the environment variable successfully overrides the file setting.
6. Cleans up the temporary resources after the test.

crates/brokkr-utils/tests/integration.rs.name: test_settings_default
crates/brokkr-utils/tests/integration.rs.doc_comment:
Tests the loading of default settings when no configuration file is provided.

This test:
1. Calls Settings::new() with None as the config file path.
2. Verifies that the default settings are correctly loaded.
3. Checks specific default values for database URL and log level.

Note: This test assumes knowledge of the expected default values. If defaults
change, this test will need to be updated accordingly.

tests/e2e/src/main.rs.doc_comment:
Brokkr End-to-End Test Suite

Holistic tests that exercise the entire Brokkr system as a user would.
These tests mirror the UI walkthrough in examples/ui-slim/DEMO_WALKTHROUGH.md

Prerequisites:
- Full Brokkr stack running (broker, agent, postgres, k3s)
- Admin PAK available via ADMIN_PAK environment variable

Run with: angreal tests e2e

tests/e2e/src/scenarios.rs.name: test_build_work_orders
tests/e2e/src/scenarios.rs.doc_comment:
Test build work orders using Shipwright.

This test creates a real build work order and waits for the agent to
process it through Shipwright. Requires:
- A running agent (brokkr-integration-test-agent)
- Tekton and Shipwright installed in the cluster
- ClusterBuildStrategies available (buildah)

tests/e2e/src/scenarios.rs.name: test_agent_reconciliation_existing_deployments
tests/e2e/src/scenarios.rs.doc_comment:
Test that agents can reconcile pre-existing deployments when targeted to a stack.
This tests the scenario where:
1. Stack exists with deployment object
2. Agent is created and targeted to the stack AFTER deployment exists
3. Agent should see the deployment via target-state API
